Title: Three
Speaker: {operative.name}
Setting: Office balcony, twenty-second floor, {location} skyline bleeding neon through smog
TextLines: {operative.name} stands at the railing, lighter open, cigarette unlit.
"That's three, now. The last one had a kid's drawing on {gender:m|his|her} desk."
The wind takes the lighter flame before it catches.

Choices.Label: "You did the job. That's what we pay for."
"You don't have to like it."
"You want a bonus or a transfer?"

Title: Performance Review
Speaker: {operative.name}
Setting: Glass conference room, Director's floor, city light refracting through rain-streaked smart-glass
TextLines: {operative.name}'s file is open on the table. Seven entries. The last three don't even have names.
"You don't ask anymore," the Director observes. "You used to ask."
{operative.name} doesn't look up from the coffee.

Choices.Label: "Promote {gender:m|him|her}. {gender:m|He's|She's} earned it."
"Bench {gender:m|him|her}. Get {gender:m|him|her} an evaluation."

Title: Empty
Speaker: {operative.name}
Setting: Operative's apartment, blackout mode, smart-home unresponsive, {location} glow leaking through seams in the blinds
TextLines: {operative.name} hasn't answered the comm in two days.
When you arrive the apartment is empty of personality — no photos, no books, no signs of life.
{operative.name} is sitting on the floor against the wall, looking at the door.

Choices.Label: Pull {gender:m|him|her} from rotation.
Tell {gender:m|him|her} tomorrow's mission needs {gender:m|him|her}.

Title: Late Edition
Speaker: Director
Setting: Director's office, wall-screen muted, news crawl cycling in silent red
TextLines: The Quill pushed the story at 0400 — algorithmic amplification had it front-of-feed by 0600. Two million impressions before breakfast.
"There's a name attached," the Director says. "Not yours yet. But the metadata trail is warm."

{gender:m|She|He} flicks the wall-screen toward you. The face is the contractor your team buried two cycles ago — reconstructed from dental records and a traffic cam still that should have been scrubbed.

Choices.Label: "Buy the journalist out."
"Bury the journalist."
"Ride it out."

Title: Welcome to the Floor
Speaker: Director
Setting: Operations bullpen, reinforced glass and matte-black consoles, holo-feeds cycling threat assessments in {corp} cyan
TextLines: "You've read the file. You know what we do. What the corporate charter calls 'asset realignment' and what the street calls something shorter."
"What you don't know yet is what it does to the people you send. The augments don't buffer that part."
"Try not to find out the hard way. This city eats the ones who learn slow."

Choices.Label: Acknowledge.

Title: Loyalty Audit
Speaker: {operative.name}
Setting: Unlicensed noodle bar, twenty blocks from any {corp} subnet, rain drumming on corrugated steel
TextLines: {operative.name} chose the meeting place — off-grid, no cameras, no corp RF signatures within three blocks. That alone tells you something.
"I want a transfer," {operative.name} says, chopsticks untouched. "Or a contract review. Pick one. Before someone else makes me a better offer."

Choices.Label: Promise the transfer.
Refuse. Quietly.

Title: Wire
Speaker: {operative.name}
Setting: Operations floor, 3 AM, fluorescent hum
TextLines: {operative.name}'s hands won't stop shaking. The mission brief is open on the desk but the words aren't landing.
"I can hear the comms even when they're off," {operative.name} says. "Every door sounds like a breach."
The coffee is untouched. It went cold hours ago.
{operative.role:anchor} is watching from the far desk, saying nothing.

Choices.Label: "Stand down. That's an order."
"We need you tomorrow. Hold it together."
"Talk to me. Off the record."

Title: External Pressure
Speaker: Director
Setting: Boardroom anteroom, electrochromic glass opaqued, sound-dampening field humming at the threshold
TextLines: "The Razor is moving on us. Open file. Public-facing. They've got satellite feeds and transaction logs."

The Director's voice is even, but {gender:m|her|his} hands are flat on the brushed-steel table — {gender:m|she's|he's} keeping them still. The holo-display behind {gender:m|her|him} cycles through intercept transcripts.
"Whatever you have planned this cycle, halve it. We go dark until the drones stop circling."

Choices.Label: Acknowledge.

Title: The Ledger
Speaker: {operative.role:conscience}
Setting: Basement archive, {corp} records wing, {year}
TextLines: {operative.role:conscience} is waiting for you in the archive. The lights flicker — nobody comes down here.
"I pulled the operational summaries. All of them." A stack of folders, thicker than it should be.
"We crossed a line somewhere, {gender:m|sir|ma'am}. I can't tell you when because there wasn't a single moment. It was all of them."
From upstairs, you hear {operative.role:weapon} laughing at something. The sound carries wrong down here.

Choices.Label: "We do what the mission requires. That hasn't changed."
"Pull the worst cases. I want a review."
"Burn those files. Now."

Title: Fracture Lines
Speaker: {operative.name}
Setting: Briefing room, mag-locked, privacy film activated, every surface reflecting strip-LED amber
TextLines: {operative.name} won't sit. Keeps pacing the length of the window.
"I'm not working with {operative.role:mirror} again. I don't care what the board says."
"Last op, {gender:m|he|she} left me in the corridor for six minutes. Six. You know what six minutes means in the field."
The request is on your desk already. Transfer or termination. {operative.name} wants an answer today.

Choices.Label: "I'll separate you. Different rotations."
"You'll work with who I assign. That's final."
"Tell me what happened. The real version."

Title: The Offer
Speaker: {operative.name}
Setting: Parking sublevel 3, autonomous vehicle idling on battery, perimeter cameras looped
TextLines: The message came through a dead drop — encrypted burst, routed through three proxy nodes. Not your dead drop. {operative.name} met someone on the other side of it.

"{faction.name} offered me a package," {operative.name} says, not looking at you. Staring at the parking sublevel's flickering sodium light. "New biometrics. New identity. Double salary. Off-grid relocation."
The vehicle hums on battery. {operative.role:climber} doesn't know about this meeting. Nobody in the building does. The camera loop buys you twelve minutes.
"I'm telling you because I haven't decided yet. And because the clock on their offer expires at midnight."

Choices.Label: "What would it take to stay?"
"If you walk, you know what happens."
"Go. I'll give you a twelve-hour head start."

Title: Dead Air
Speaker: {operative.role:anchor}
Setting: Operations bullpen, end of shift, half the workstations dark, holo-screens cycling standby patterns
TextLines: The bullpen used to hum at shift change — arguments, laughter, the clatter of someone's takeout hitting a desk. Now the loudest sound is the climate system cycling.
{operative.role:anchor} catches you at the door. "We're hemorrhaging, {gender:m|sir|ma'am}. Not bodies. Something worse."
"{operative.role:wildcard} hasn't pinged the group chat in three days. {operative.role:survivor} is filling out transfer forms — I saw it on their screen before they killed the display."
"They're not angry. Angry I can work with. They've gone flat. Like the building ate whatever was left."

Choices.Label: "Organize something. Off-book. Dinner, drinks, I don't care."
"Cut the dead weight. Reassign anyone below baseline."
"They'll find their footing. Give it time."

Title: Rooftop
Speaker: {operative.name}
Setting: Building rooftop, {location}, city noise far below
TextLines: No ops running. No comms traffic. The board is clean for the first time in weeks.
{operative.name} found you up here. Brought two cups of something that passes for coffee in {location}.
"You ever think about what happens after?" {operative.name} asks. Not fishing. Just asking.
The city hums. {operative.role:mirror} is down in the bullpen, probably reading the same blank board.
For a few minutes, nobody needs anything from you.

Choices.Label: "After? There's no after in this line of work."
"I think about it more than I should."
Say nothing. Drink the coffee.

Title: Competent
Speaker: Director Vale
Setting: Corner office, morning light filtered through smart-glass, {location} skyline pixelated by atmospheric haze
TextLines: Vale closes a holo-folder with a flick. Your name glows on the tab in {corp} corporate blue.
"The board's analytics flagged your division. Clean operations, minimal exposure, acceptable losses. Your efficiency rating is in the top decile."
A pause. The kind that means the next sentence was rehearsed.
"They're routing you harder contracts. Ones the other divisions won't touch. Consider it a compliment. Or a test. The difference doesn't matter up here."

Choices.Label: "I'll handle whatever comes."
"Define 'harder.'"

Title: Effective
Speaker: Director Vale
Setting: Executive dining room, biometric locks, {location} glittering forty stories below, empty except for two place settings
TextLines: Vale pours something from a bottle with no label — real grapes, not synth. Worth more than a field operative's quarterly bonus.
"Your people are efficient. The board's AI is using words like 'reliable' and 'dependable.' In this company, those words mean something specific. They mean the guardrails are coming off."
"It means they'll start routing you work that doesn't come with written orders. Jobs that exist in the space between what the charter permits and what the market requires."
Somewhere below, {operative.role:conscience} is probably still at their desk. Still believing the mission statements on the lobby wall — the ones written in chrome script that nobody reads anymore.

Choices.Label: "I understand the arrangement."
"My team follows policy. So do I."
"What's the upside?"

Title: Feared
Setting: Elevator, between floors, camera disabled
TextLines: The elevator stops between floors. Not a malfunction. A conversation.
A voice from the board's security detail: "Your division's reputation precedes it. Rival divisions are requesting transfers out. Three this quarter."
"The board finds this... useful. Fear is a resource, and you've learned to spend it."
The elevator resumes. {operative.role:weapon} is waiting in the lobby, reading something on their phone. They don't look up when the doors open.

Choices.Label: Fear is just efficiency with better PR.
This isn't what I built.

Title: The Machine
Setting: Your office. Late. The lights are off but you haven't noticed.
TextLines: The org chart on your wall has more red lines than black ones now. Terminated. Transferred. Reassigned. The euphemisms have their own euphemisms.
You realise you've been sitting in the dark for forty minutes, reviewing personnel files by screen light. Not reading them. Processing them.
{operative.role:mirror} knocked earlier. You heard it. You chose not to answer.
The building hums. Climate control, server cooling, security systems. It sounds like breathing. It sounds like you.

Choices.Label: Close the file. Open the next one.
Turn on the lights.

TextLines: You don't remember when the names stopped mattering.
The reports are clean. The numbers are good. The board is satisfied.
Somewhere in the building, someone is having the worst day of their life because of a decision you made before your second coffee.
You didn't make it to hurt them. You didn't make it not to. You just made it.
This is Tuesday.

Choices.Label: Next.

Title: Neon Wash
Speaker: {operative.name}
Setting: Sublevel conference room, strip-LEDs cycling indigo to magenta, rain streaking the glass ceiling
TextLines: The projector throws mission telemetry across the far wall — thermal overlays, comm intercepts, satellite feeds — all of it rendered in {corp}'s proprietary cyan.
{operative.name} hasn't sat down yet. Still in field blacks, still smelling like ozone and cheap synth-rain from the {location} underpass.
"Debrief says clean. My hands say otherwise."
The after-action report blinks on your tablet. Three paragraphs of operational language that don't mention the guard who stopped breathing in the stairwell.
Outside, the arcology hums its sixty-hertz lullaby. Business as usual.

Choices.Label: "Clean is what the report says. That's what clean means here."
"Walk me through it. The parts that aren't in the file."
"File it. Shower. Sleep. Debrief's over."

Title: Overflow
Speaker: {operative.role:anchor}
Setting: Rooftop bar, forty floors up, {location} skyline bleeding neon into low cloud
TextLines: Someone booked the rooftop. Expensed it to operational morale — line item nobody audits.

{operative.role:anchor} is two drinks ahead of everyone, telling a version of the Kasumi extraction that didn't happen but should have. {operative.role:wildcard} is laughing hard enough to spill something expensive on the railing.
The {location} sprawl stretches out below — a circuit board someone left powered on overnight. Traffic drones weave between ad-blimps pushing pharma and corpo recruitment.
For one night, nobody's asset is anybody's liability.
Your comm is off. You checked twice.

Choices.Label: Stay. Buy the next round.
One drink. Then back to the floor.
Watch from the doorway. Don't sit down.

Title: Signal Loss
Speaker: {operative.name}
Setting: Medical sublevel, fluorescent buzz, antiseptic and burnt polymer
TextLines: {operative.name} was found in the server room at 0400, pulling cable. Methodically. Disconnecting comms arrays one junction at a time.
Security didn't intervene — they called you.
The med-tech's report glows on the wall-screen behind {operative.name}'s head: elevated cortisol, sleep debt measured in weeks, micro-tremors in both hands.
"{gender:m|He|She} keeps saying the signal is wrong," the tech whispers. "That the frequencies don't match the mission logs."
{operative.name} looks at you with perfect clarity. That's the part that worries you.

Choices.Label: "Pull {gender:m|him|her} from active. Full psychiatric evaluation."
"Sedate {gender:m|him|her}. Wipe the security footage. This didn't happen."
"Let me talk to {gender:m|him|her}. Alone."

Title: Chrome and Courtesy
Setting: Neutral ground — the Parallax Lounge, {location}, privacy field active
TextLines: The invitation came on actual paper — cream stock, embossed with the Kasumi Dynamics seal. Old-world power move.
Their handler is already seated when you arrive. Chrome jaw implant, tailored graphene suit, drink untouched. The privacy field turns the rest of the lounge into soft static.
"Your division has been... energetic. Our actuaries have noticed."
A data-slate slides across the table. Casualty projections. Yours and theirs. The numbers are not kind to either side.
"I'm here to discuss whether those numbers need to get worse, or whether we can find a more profitable arrangement."

Choices.Label: "Show me the terms. I'm listening."
"Your actuaries should update their models. We're just getting started."
"This meeting never happened. Enjoy your drink."

Title: Glass Ceiling
Speaker: Board Chair Okonkwo
Setting: 67th floor boardroom, panoramic windows, {location} at altitude
TextLines: Seven chairs. Five occupied. The empty two belong to members who voted against this hearing.
Okonkwo's face is a mask of corporate patience — the kind that costs six figures in executive coaching. The holographic minutes float between you.
"Your division's operational expenditure has exceeded projected returns by a factor of three. Your heat index is a liability the insurance underwriters have flagged. Twice."
A pause calibrated to the millisecond.
"Convince us you're still an asset and not a line item waiting for deletion."
Somewhere below, {operative.role:conscience} is probably updating their resume.

Choices.Label: "The numbers don't capture what we prevent. I have case files."
"Cut my budget in half. I'll deliver the same results."
"Fire me. See what happens to your containment metrics."

Title: Archive
Speaker: Director Vale
Setting: Vale's private office, old wood under new glass, bourbon older than the arcology
TextLines: Vale pours two glasses without asking. The bottle has no label — pre-collapse stock, worth more than most operatives' annual salary.
"I had a handler once. Before your time, before this title. She told me something I didn't understand until I'd been doing this for a decade."
The ice shifts in the glass. Outside, a cargo drone slides past the window trailing position lights.
"She said: 'The job doesn't change you. It reveals you. And most people don't like the reveal.'"
Vale looks at you the way people look at X-rays.
"So. What has it revealed so far?"

Choices.Label: "That I'm good at this. Better than I should be."
"That I can live with the math. Most days."
"Ask me again in another ten cycles."

Title: Fresh Chrome
Setting: Hiring floor, {corp} talent acquisition, holographic candidate profiles rotating
TextLines: Talent acquisition sent three files. Headshots glow on the interview room's curved display — all of them young, all of them hungry, none of them knowing what the job actually entails.
The first candidate has mil-spec reflex augments and a discharge that reads 'administrative' in the kind of font that means 'classified.'
The second is a self-taught decker out of the {location} undercity. No formal record before age nineteen. That's either impressive or fabricated.
The third has a recommendation letter from someone in the Finance Division. That alone tells you everything about whose interests they serve.
You have budget for one. Maybe.

Choices.Label: "Send me the soldier. We need hands, not questions."
"The decker. We're exposed on the net and we both know it."
"The connected one. Better to have Finance's eyes where I can see them."

Title: Black Ice
Speaker: {operative.name}
Setting: Operations floor, emergency lighting, every screen red
TextLines: The comms went dead at 02:17. By 02:19 every alarm on the operations floor was screaming.

{operative.name} made it back. Barely. Field blacks torn at the shoulder, blood — not all {gender:m|his|hers} — streaked across the collar. The extraction vehicle is still burning three blocks from the {location} waterfront.
{operative.role:anchor} is running triage on the debrief data, pulling what they can from corrupted helmet feeds.
"We were made before we breached the perimeter," {operative.name} says, voice flat. "Someone fed them our approach vector. Exact coordinates. Exact timing."
The implication hangs in the emergency light like smoke.

Choices.Label: "Lock down all comms. Full security audit. Nobody leaves this floor."
"Get {gender:m|him|her} to medical. We'll figure out who talked later."
"Pull the access logs. I want names before sunrise."

Title: Dead Channel
Setting: Your office, {location}, rain on glass, city light refracted into slow geometry
TextLines: No operations running. No comms traffic. The mission board is empty for the first time in weeks and the silence has a weight to it, like pressure equalization.

The rain draws lines down the floor-to-ceiling glass. Below, the {location} night shift moves through neon and vapor — delivery drones, late-shift workers, the occasional patrol vehicle sweeping its searchlight across wet pavement.
Your desk is clean. The reports are filed. The coffee is cold but you drink it anyway.
There's a photo in the second drawer you haven't looked at in months. You think about opening it.
The building breathes. The servers hum. The rain continues.
Somewhere in this city, nobody needs you to make a decision that ruins their life. Not tonight.

Choices.Label: Open the drawer.
Leave it closed. Go home.
Stay. Pull tomorrow's briefs early.

Title: Terminus
Setting: Sub-basement server farm, {corp} central archive, cold enough to see your breath
TextLines: The archive is three stories of server racks descending into bedrock. Climate control keeps it just above freezing. Your breath fogs under strip lighting that hasn't been serviced since the building was new.

{operative.role:conscience} is waiting at the bottom. They have a drive — a small one, matte black, no markings. Everything your division has done is on it. Every operation, every casualty, every line item the board never audited.
"I'm walking this out of the building," {operative.role:conscience} says. Not angry. Not afraid. Resolved.
"The Razor gets it by morning. Or the press. Or both. I haven't decided."
Above you, {operative.role:weapon} is stationed at the only exit. They're waiting for your call.
The servers hum. The cold sinks through your shoes. The drive is small enough to crush in one hand.

Choices.Label: "Give me the drive. Walk away. I'll make sure you disappear clean."
"Take it. Go. I won't stop you."
Signal {operative.role:weapon}.
